% Helper function to calculate prefix maximums for a list. O(N).
prefix_max(L) when is_list(L) ->
    case L of
        [] -> [];
        [H|T] ->
            % scanl computes the running maximum, starting with H.
            lists:scanl(fun(current_max, next_element) -> max(current_max, next_element) end, H, T)
    end.

% Helper function to calculate suffix minimums for a list. O(N).
% RMin[i] = min(nums[i..n-1])
suffix_min(L) when is_list(L) ->
    case L of
        [] -> [];
        _ ->
            % 1. Reverse the list: L_rev = [n_{N-1}, ..., n_0]
            L_rev = lists:reverse(L),
            % 2. Calculate prefix minimums on L_rev.
            % scanl starting with n_{N-1} produces:
            % [RMin[N-1], RMin[N-2], ..., RMin[0]]
            R_scan = lists:scanl(fun(current_min, next_element) -> min(current_min, next_element) end, lists:head(L_rev), L_rev),
            % 3. Reverse the result back to obtain [RMin[0], RMin[1], ..., RMin[N-1]].
            lists:reverse(R_scan)
    end.

% Main solver function
solve(Nums, K) when is_list(Nums) ->
    case N of
        0 -> -1; % Should not happen based on constraints, but good practice
        _ ->
            LMax = prefix_max(Nums),
            RMin = suffix_min(Nums),
            % Iterate through the lists simultaneously using recursion, keeping track of the index.
            find_smallest_stable_index(LMax, RMin, K, 0)
    end
where
    N = length(Nums).

% Recursive helper to check the stability condition
find_smallest_stable_index(_LMax, _RMin, _K, _Index) when _Index >= N -> -1;
find_smallest_stable_index([LMax|TLMax], [RMin|TRMin], K, I) ->
    % Instability value = LMax[I] - RMin[I]
    InstabilityValue = LMax - RMin,
    if InstabilityValue =< K ->
        I % Found the smallest stable index
    ;
        % Continue checking the next index
        find_smallest_stable_index(TLMax, TRMin, K, I + 1)
    end;
find_smallest_stable_index([], [], _K, _) -> -1. % Should be covered by the first clause, but safe guard

solve(Nums, K) ->
    case Nums of
        [] -> -1;
        _ ->
            % 1. Calculate Prefix Maximums (PMax)
            PMax = pmax_list(Nums),

            % 2. Calculate Suffix Minimums (SMin)
            SMin = smin_list(Nums),

            % 3. Find the smallest stable index
            find_stable_index(PMax, SMin, K, 0)
    end.

% Helper function to calculate Prefix Maximums
% Returns a list where the i-th element is max(Nums[0..i])
pmax_list(Nums) ->
    case Nums of
        [] -> [];
        [H|T] ->
            % pmax_rec(RemainingList, CurrentMax, Accumulator)
            pmax_rec(T, H, [H])
    end.

pmax_rec([], _, Acc) -> lists:reverse(Acc).
pmax_rec([H|T], CurrentMax, Acc) ->
    NewMax = max(H, CurrentMax),
    pmax_rec(T, NewMax, [NewMax|Acc]).

% Helper function to calculate Suffix Minimums
% Returns a list where the i-th element is min(Nums[i..N-1])
smin_list(Nums) ->
    case Nums of
        [] -> [];
        [_] -> Nums;
        [H|T] ->
            % smin_backwards computes SMin recursively from right to left
            SMinT = smin_backwards(T),
            % SMinT is already in the correct order (SMin[1]...SMin[N-1])

    case T of

            SMinT = smin_backwards(T),
            [SMinT_H | SMinT_Rest] = SMinT,
